title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Suezmax West Africa to UK Continent
Remarkable rise of rates and tonne days growth with the onset of Middle East geopolitical crisis
The crude freight market gained momentum in early November due to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. The VLCC segment has seen significant activity, but the Suezmax and Aframax segments have experienced even more growth compared to their levels at the end of September. The Suezmax segment, where shipments usually run from West Africa to the Continent, has recorded one of the most substantial growth spikes of 2021.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Suezmax West Africa to UK Continent
section_path: Chart of the Week: Suezmax West Africa to UK Continent
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Suezmax West Africa to UK Continent
children.summary:
Remarkable rise of rates and tonne days growth with the onset of Middle East geopolitical crisis
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T102700.594.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t102700594_27b5b4df4455.png
The crude freight market gained momentum in early November due to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. The VLCC segment has seen significant activity, but the Suezmax and Aframax segments have experienced even more growth compared to their levels at the end of September.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Firmer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104201.839.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104201839_efd9976d17f4.png
Firmer crude oil freight rate momentum in early November suggests a sharp rise in rates given geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. VLCC MEG-China freight rates increased to 73 WS, up 38% from the previous week and down 25% from a year ago.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR2 Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104341.490.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104341490_b22e6c93264b.png
LR2 AG freight rates fell to 160 WS, down 7% from the previous week but up 18% from a month ago.

children.title: Panamax Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104504.244.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104504244_d69a3d52b120.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates recorded a significant firmer momentum of the previous week and recent levels reached WS 250, 90% more than a month ago.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104656.011.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104656011_136686a09b0e.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent rose to WS 308, up 30% from a month ago. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
'Dirty' (#vessels) - Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104741.744.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104741744_2d343986366b.png
With signs of an increase in VLCC for the Ras Tanura, November began with a downward correction in the other ship size segments. VLCC Ras Tanura: The number of ships has increased to 54, 8 below the annual average, with a downward trend for early November.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104848.753.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104848753_2d9c4ff8830b.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T104945.047.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t104945047_f8085e50d142.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of recorded ships has remained below the year's average but with signs of an increase. The recent figure reached 7, nearly 4 above the end of October.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T105107.479.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t105107479_7edacfafb89e.png
Dirty tonne days: The outlook for early November confirmed the previous week's picture for the Aframax segment, where the trend has been clearly upward since the end of week 40. In addition, there also seems to be a slight upward correction in VLCC, which will be confirmed in the coming days.

children.title: ‘Clean' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-03T105222.195.png | assets/2023-11-03_siganl-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-03t105222195_5bb6c9b3c84d.png
Panamax tonne days: The growth rate fell again, contrary to expectations, as the last days of October showed signs of improvement. Clean MR tonne days: The outlook for demand growth has weakened further, and there are clearer signs of a decline in MR1 size.
